name: Barbecued Salmon with Field Greens
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Olive oil reserved from rub
recipeInstructions:
From Will Greenwood, not-quite White House chef.
For the rub: Combine chili, garlic and onion powders, sugar, salt, allspice, cumin, white pepper and paprika in a food processor until thoroughly mixed. Sprinkle all but 1 1/2 tablespoons of the rub on all sides of the salmon. Let fillets marinate for 6 hours.
To cook the salmon: Prepare a very hot grill. If using wood chips on the hot coals, first soak these in water so they will smoke more. Add soaked chips 5 minutes before starting the salmon. Rub fillets with a thin film of olive oil. Grill them approximately 8-10 minutes, depending on thickness.
For the field greens and vinaigrette: Clean and refresh the greens in cold water. Spin dry. Mix vegetable juice, vinegar and tomato together with the reserved barbecue rub and olive oil. Toss with the greens.
To serve: Divide greens among plates. Top each plate with a salmon fillet. Spoon the remaining vinaigrette over the salmon. Serve hot.
